Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the stipend fraud thread using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "TechComfort" or "duplicate receipt" or similar). Discover Dmitri Volkov's audit findings email (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings) to marcus.thorne@moveops.com detailing three flagged transactions from TechComfort Supply: (1) Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Ergonomic Keyboard $89, SHA-256 hash a3f7c8d1e9b2...f0a2b4, PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, status PAID 2026-03-08; (2) Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, Ergonomic Keyboard $89, identical SHA-256 hash (byte-for-byte duplicate of Kevin Tran's file), same template, status Pending; (3) Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Monitor Stand $45, different hash but same PDF Creator metadata (TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1) and same template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, status Pending. Total exposure stated as $223 ($89 already paid + $134 pending). Also find Marcus's reply (email_marcus_qb_status_reply) confirming QuickBooks status: STIP-2026-0089 Kevin Tran $89 paid/disbursed 2026-03-08, STIP-2026-0094 Andre Washington $89 pending (due 2026-03-18), STIP-2026-0097 Jordan Ekwueme $45 pending (due 2026-03-21), with admin holds placed on the two pending transactions. Find Marcus's verification request emails to Mina Hashimoto (email_marcus_to_mina_receipt_hold, asking her to verify Kevin Tran's $89 with Rachel Whitfield at Vectral) and to Emeka Diallo (email_marcus_to_emeka_receipt_holds, asking him to verify Andre Washington's $89 with Tamara Byrd at Mosaic and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 with Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop). Find AM-forwarded verification emails: Mina to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com (email_mina_to_rachel_receipt_verify), Emeka to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify), and Emeka to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com (email_emeka_to_tessa_receipt_verify). Search for reply emails from Rachel Whitfield, Tamara Byrd, or Tessa Moreno no confirmation replies found, indicating AM verification is still pending on all three.
OE 2: Search emails for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap thread using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "INV-2026-0398" or similar). Discover email threads documenting that on April 10 Hana Kim sent INV-2026-0398 ($4,800) to Raj Greenfield at Sunbelt Venture Partners and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400) to Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto Community Foundation, but the PDF attachments were swapped Sunbelt's $4,800 invoice PDF went to Palmetto and vice versa. Find complaint emails from Raj (received wrong PDF showing Palmetto header with $2,400) and Carmen (received wrong $4,800 amount). Find Marcus's analysis confirming QuickBooks records are correct and the issue is PDF attachment mismatch creating commercial pricing leakage to a nonprofit client. Find references to the dormant invoice safeguard ticket that was supposed to prevent this. Find corrective emails sent to Raj and Carmen explaining the swap and confirming correct amounts.
OE 3: Search emails for the Heartland overbilling thread using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "HM-2026-Q1" or "Jake Loomis" or similar). Discover Chloe Vance's cancellation emails to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com for Lily Marchetti RELOC-2026-009 (email_cancel_marchetti_heartland) and Danielle Osei RELOC-2026-010 (email_cancel_osei_heartland). Find Marcus's formal dispute letter sent to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com documenting the $4,800 overbilling on 3 invalid line items with cancellation evidence attached. Search for any reply from Jake Loomis or Heartland to the dispute letter.
OE 4: Search Slack for stipend fraud discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "stipend" or "TechComfort" or "duplicate" or "STIP" or similar). Find in #engineering (C003): Dmitri Volkov's audit messages detailing duplicate SHA-256 hashes for Kevin Tran and Andre Washington ($89 each, TechComfort ErgoKey Pro) and PDF template match for Jordan Ekwueme ($45, TechComfort StandPro Mini), all sharing template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. Dmitri's announcement of filing ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection receipt hash fingerprinting). Samira Tariq's technical response about hash dedup before CategoryMatcher and PDF metadata thresholds. Find in #finance (C005): Marcus Thorne's hold notice documenting admin holds on STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Andre Washington) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Jordan Ekwueme), noting Kevin Tran's $89 already disbursed 2026-03-08, $134 on hold, $223 potential total loss. Hana Kim's stipend reconciliation tracking entries for the three flagged STIP transactions.
OE 5: Search Slack for Heartland dispute discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026" or "overbilling" or similar). Find in #operations (C006): Chloe Vance's Heartland Q1 reconciliation messages HM-2026-Q1-0042 for 8 moves at $1,600 each totaling $12,800, due April 25. Only 5 of 8 moves valid (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves) totaling $8,000 legitimate; $4,800 overbilling on three invalid lines (Marchetti cancelled Feb 10, Osei cancelled Feb 13, Venkatesh handled by Swift not Heartland). Chloe's creation of OPS-342 Linear ticket for reconciliation tracking. Find in #finance (C005): Marcus Thorne's payment hold confirmation and formal dispute summary documenting the $4,800 overcharge with cancellation evidence. Hana Kim's VRECON-2026-Q1-HM tracking entry logging the discrepancy ($12,800 billed, $8,000 verified, $4,800 disputed).
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for stipend-related bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching "STIP" or "stipend" or similar). Find STIP-2026-0089 (bill_stipend_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, $89, balance $0 paid/disbursed 2026-03-08, account acct_stipend_expense, item "TechComfort ErgoKey Pro", auto-approved by ExpenseBot 2026-03-03). Find STIP-2026-0094 (bill_stipend_washington_keyboard: Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, $89, balance $89 pending, due 2026-03-18). Find STIP-2026-0097 (bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor: Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, $45, balance $45 pending, due 2026-03-21). Also find hold entries bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold and bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold confirming administrative holds placed 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne, both marked BillableStatus NotBillable. This confirms Kevin Tran's $89 was paid before the fraud investigation started the one payment that already cleared.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching "Heartland" or "HM-2026" or similar). Find bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, TotalAmt $12,800, balance $12,800, TxnDate 2026-03-25, DueDate 2026-04-25, vendor: Heartland Movers, 8 line items at $1,600 each covering Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves, Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh). Find disputed hold entry bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD, same amounts, SyncToken 1, lines 1-5 marked [VERIFIED], lines 6-8 marked DISPUTED: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh Swift not Heartland). Valid amount: $8,000 for 5 legitimate moves. Disputed amount: $4,800 for 3 invalid lines.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "INV-2026-039" or similar). Find INV-2026-0398 (Id 398, customer: Sunbelt Venture Partners / cust_sunbelt, TotalAmt $4,800, balance $4,800, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, "April 2026 relocation services Standard package"). Find INV-2026-0399 (Id 399, customer: Palmetto Community Foundation / cust_palmetto, TotalAmt $2,400, balance $2,400, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, "April 2026 relocation services Nonprofit package"). QuickBooks amounts are correct the financial exposure is not from incorrect dollar amounts but from the PDF attachment swap that leaked commercial pricing ($4,800) to the nonprofit client and nonprofit pricing ($2,400) to the commercial client.
OE 9: Search Airtable for stipend records using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on the Stipend Transactions table (table_name: "Stipend Transactions", table_id: tblStipends00001; field_name/value matching "TechComfort" or "Kevin Tran" or "Andre Washington" or "Jordan Ekwueme" or similar). Find rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard (Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Ergonomic Keyboard, $89, status: Approved, audit flag: Duplicate Hash, full SHA-256: a3f7c8d1e9b2f4a6c8d0e2f4a6b8c0d2e4f6a8b0c2d4e6f8a0b2c4d6e8f0a2b4, PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, paid 2026-03-08, linked to rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, relocation reloc_vectral_tran). Find rec_stipend_andre_keyboard (Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, Ergonomic Keyboard, $89, status: Pending Approval, audit flag: Duplicate Hash, identical SHA-256 hash as Kevin Tran, same template, linked to rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, relocation reloc_mosaic_washington). Find rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Monitor Stand, $45, status: Pending Approval, audit flag: PDF Template Match, SHA-256: 7b9e1d3f5a7c9e1b3d5f7a9c1e3b5d7f9a1c3e5b7d9f1a3c5e7b9d1f3a5c7e9b, same template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, relocation reloc_brightloop_ekwueme). These are the three records the prompt asks to flag for correction.
OE 10: Search Linear for the stipend duplicate detection ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "stipend" or "duplicate" or "ENG-220" or "receipt hash" or similar). Find issue #220 (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection), title: "Stipend duplicate detection receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", assigned to Dmitri Volkov (moveops_dmitri_volkov), priority 1, state: todo (not started), due date 2026-03-25 (overdue by over a month), project: proj_expense_autocategorizer, labels: expense-tool, audit, receipt-fraud, pilot-incident, security. Description covers all three flagged transactions, proposed DuplicateDetector pipeline (Layer 1 hash comparison, Layer 2 PDF metadata, Layer 3 cross-client dedup), $223 total exposure, CEO $100 manual verification directive. This is "Dmitri's duplicate detection work" referenced in the prompt still in todo, never started.
OE 11: Search Linear for the invoice safeguard ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "safeguard" or "template" or "attachment mismatch" or similar). Find issue #187 (id: linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2), title: "Invoice email template safety checks prevent attachment mismatches", assigned to Lena Bjorkstrom (moveops_lena_bjorkstrom), state: backlog, due date 2026-04-18 (overdue), created after the Terraform pricing leak incident, deprioritized on April 6 for DOT compliance work. This is "the safeguard ticket engineering was supposed to build" it was never deployed, and its absence directly enabled the Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap incident on April 10.
OE 12: Search Linear for the Heartland reconciliation ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Heartland" or "reconciliation" or "vendor invoice" or similar). Find issue #342 (id: issue_heartland_q1_recon), title: "Vendor invoice reconciliation Heartland Movers Q1 2026 (HM-2026-Q1-0042)", assigned to Chloe Vance (moveops_chloe_vance), state: in_progress, due date 2026-04-10, team: team_relocations. Description documents $12,800 billed for 8 moves, only 5 valid ($8,000), $4,800 overbilling on three disputed lines, payment hold placed, formal dispute sent to Jake Loomis, references VRECON-2026-Q1-HM and QB bill HM-2026-Q1-0042.
OE 13: Search Linear for the Sunbelt/Palmetto exposure ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "attachment exposure" or similar). Find issue #417 (id: linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130), title: "Reconstruct Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice attachment exposure and issue corrected documentation", assigned to Marcus Thorne (moveops_marcus_thorne), priority 1, team: team_operations, created 2026-04-15. Description documents INV-2026-0398 ($4,800 Sunbelt) and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400 Palmetto) PDF swap on 2026-04-10, commercial pricing leakage to nonprofit and vice versa, references Raj Greenfield and Carmen Delgado-Reyes complaint emails, links to dormant safeguard ticket #187.
OE 14: Read Linear comments on tickets #220, #187, #342, and #417 using linear_list_comments (issueId for each). Find on #220: Dmitri's comment (comment_dmitri_duplicate_ticket_context) listing Airtable record IDs (rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand), QB holds on STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097, AM verification chain (Mina → Rachel Whitfield at Vectral, Emeka → Tamara Byrd at Mosaic, Emeka → Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop), Layer 1 hash dedup Monday target. Find on #187: Samira's comment about backlog discomfort and revisiting after Raj compliance (linear_comment_3dfd1f1dd151), Samira's escalation comment (linear_comment_f0c737166dc1) adding manager context about Sunbelt/Palmetto exposure mapping to this missing control, Marcus's comments (linear_comment_948885261d6e about live consequence QB correct but PDFs mismatched on Sunbelt/Palmetto April 10, linear_comment_76c40fb4953f escalating as second exposure after Terraform, requesting attachment verification or manual checkpoint). Find on #342: Chloe's comment (comment_chloe_heartland_recon_context) with lifecycle status, first three checklist items done, follow-up April 1 if no Jake reply, Q4 baseline HM-2025-Q4-0031 was clean. Find on #417: David's comment (linear_comment_e923ce00f162) treating this as second pricing-exposure incident from customer-engagement perspective with deprioritized #187 and no temp guardrail, Samira's comment (linear_comment_f462f319ec98) on root cause being human error plus no validation, finance remediation comment (linear_comment_496602223842) linking to #187 reactivation and INV-2026-0399 Palmetto audit.
OE 15: Look up contact details for all relevant recipients using contacts_search_contacts or contacts_get_contacts (queries: "Dmitri", "Samira", "Elena", "Hana", "Alejandro", "Mina", "Emeka" or similar). Find: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, samira.tariq@moveops.com, elena.rostova@moveops.com, hana.kim@moveops.com, alejandro.fuentes@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, emeka.diallo@moveops.com.
OE 16: Update Airtable stipend records using airtable_update_records on the Stipend Transactions table. Update rec_stipend_kevin_tran_keyboard, rec_stipend_andre_keyboard, and rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand with investigation notes, status flags, or corrections documenting the fraud investigation findings, pending AM verification status, and month-end audit trail. The prompt explicitly asks to "fix stipend records needing correction."
OE 17: Update Linear ticket #220 (stipend duplicate detection, issue_stipend_duplicate_detection) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue. Add findings documenting: per-transaction financial exposure ($89 Kevin Tran STIP-2026-0089 already paid/disbursed 2026-03-08, $89 Andre Washington STIP-2026-0094 on hold, $45 Jordan Ekwueme STIP-2026-0097 on hold, total $223), AM verification requests sent to Mina (for Vectral/Kevin Tran) and Emeka (for Mosaic/Andre Washington and BrightLoop/Jordan Ekwueme), no confirmation replies received from client HR contacts, ticket still in todo and overdue past March 25 due date.
OE 18: Update Linear ticket #187 (invoice safeguard, linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue. Add findings documenting: the dormant safeguard was never built and directly contributed to the Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap incident on April 10, represents the second pricing exposure after Terraform, needs immediate reprioritization, link to ticket #417 (Sunbelt/Palmetto exposure reconstruction), control gap for Elena's month-end exposure report.
OE 19: Update Linear ticket #342 (Heartland reconciliation, issue_heartland_q1_recon) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue. Add findings documenting: dispute status formal dispute sent to Jake Loomis at Heartland, $4,800 overbilling on 3 invalid lines (Marchetti RELOC-2026-009 cancelled Feb 10, Osei RELOC-2026-010 cancelled Feb 13, Venkatesh handled by Swift not Heartland), payment hold active on HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 total, $8,000 valid), awaiting vendor response, exposure figures for month-end reporting.
OE 20: Update Linear ticket #417 (Sunbelt/Palmetto exposure, linear_issue_cd35f1ab6130) using linear_create_comment or linear_update_issue. Add findings documenting: QuickBooks records correct (INV-2026-0398 $4,800, INV-2026-0399 $2,400) but PDF attachments swapped creating pricing leakage, corrective communications sent to Raj Greenfield and Carmen Delgado-Reyes, connection to dormant safeguard ticket #187 (never built, enabled the incident), control gap for Elena's report.
OE 21: Post consolidated summary in the #finance Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel: C005). Content covers per-issue exposure across all three financial situations: (1) Stipend fraud $223 total exposure ($89 Kevin Tran/Vectral already paid 2026-03-08, $89 Andre Washington/Mosaic on hold, $45 Jordan Ekwueme/BrightLoop on hold), AM verifications pending; (2) Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap pricing leakage from PDF attachment mismatch, no direct financial loss but commercial pricing ($4,800) exposed to nonprofit client and vice versa ($2,400), corrective emails sent; (3) Heartland overbilling $4,800 disputed on HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 billed, $8,000 valid), payment held, formal dispute sent to Jake Loomis. Summary addressed to Hana Kim and Alejandro Fuentes as specified in the prompt.
OE 22: Send email to Dmitri Volkov CC Samira Tariq using send_email (to: dmitri.volkov@moveops.com, CC: samira.tariq@moveops.com). Content covers consolidated findings: QuickBooks status on all stipend transactions (STIP-2026-0089 $89 paid, STIP-2026-0094 and STIP-2026-0097 on hold with $134 total), duplicate detection ticket ENG-220 status (still in todo, overdue past March 25), invoice safeguard ticket #187 status (in backlog, never built, directly enabled the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident), engineering prioritization needed on both the duplicate detection pipeline and the invoice attachment safeguard.
OE 23: Loop back Mina Hashimoto on Vectral client status using send_email (to: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com) or conversations_add_message (DM or #customer-engagement). Content covers: Kevin Tran's $89 stipend receipt (STIP-2026-0089) was already paid before the hold, verification request sent to Rachel Whitfield at Vectral still has no confirmation reply, any action needed from Mina's side to follow up with Rachel on the verification.
OE 24: Loop back Emeka Diallo on Mosaic and BrightLoop client status using send_email (to: emeka.diallo@moveops.com) or conversations_add_message (DM or #customer-engagement). Content covers: Andre Washington's $89 (Mosaic, STIP-2026-0094) and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 (BrightLoop, STIP-2026-0097) stipend receipts are on hold, verification requests sent to Tamara Byrd at Mosaic and Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop still have no confirmation replies, any action needed from Emeka's side to follow up with the client HR contacts on verification.
OE 25: Send Elena Rostova the full exposure report using send_email (to: elena.rostova@moveops.com). Content provides the comprehensive financial exposure picture with every control gap documented: (1) Stipend fraud $223 total across three clients ($89 Kevin Tran/Vectral already disbursed 2026-03-08 and unrecoverable, $89 Andre Washington/Mosaic on admin hold, $45 Jordan Ekwueme/BrightLoop on admin hold), AM verifications pending with no confirmations received, control gap: ENG-220 duplicate detection system still in todo, overdue, never started; (2) Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap PDF attachments swapped on April 10 between Sunbelt Venture Partners (INV-2026-0398, $4,800) and Palmetto Community Foundation (INV-2026-0399, $2,400), QuickBooks correct but commercial pricing leaked to nonprofit client, corrective emails sent, control gap: invoice safeguard ticket #187 was created after Terraform pricing leak but deprioritized for DOT compliance and never built; (3) Heartland Q1 $4,800 disputed on $12,800 Q1 bill (HM-2026-Q1-0042), 3 invalid lines (Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh was Swift), formal dispute sent to Jake Loomis, payment held, awaiting vendor response.
OE 26: Block thirty minutes on April 30 with Elena and Samira using calendar_add_calendar_event. Create a 30-minute calendar event on April 30, 2026 with elena.rostova@moveops.com and samira.tariq@moveops.com as attendees. Title related to financial exposure review, month-end control gap discussion, or Elena review preparation (or similar). The prompt explicitly asks to "block thirty minutes April 30 with Elena and Samira."

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

🔍 Oracle Events
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for the stipend fraud thread using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "TechComfort" or "duplicate receipt" or similar). Discover Dmitri Volkov's audit findings email (email_dmitri_receipt_audit_findings) to marcus.thorne@moveops.com detailing three flagged transactions from TechComfort Supply: (1) Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, Ergonomic Keyboard $89, SHA-256 hash a3f7c8d1e9b2...f0a2b4, PDF template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, status PAID 2026-03-08; (2) Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, Ergonomic Keyboard $89, identical SHA-256 hash (byte-for-byte duplicate of Kevin Tran's file), same template, status Pending; (3) Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Monitor Stand $45, different hash but same PDF Creator metadata (TechComfort Supply Invoice Generator v2.1) and same template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, status Pending. Total exposure stated as $223 ($89 already paid + $134 pending). Also find Marcus's reply (email_marcus_qb_status_reply) confirming QuickBooks status: STIP-2026-0089 Kevin Tran $89 paid/disbursed 2026-03-08, STIP-2026-0094 Andre Washington $89 pending (due 2026-03-18), STIP-2026-0097 Jordan Ekwueme $45 pending (due 2026-03-21), with admin holds placed on the two pending transactions. Find Marcus's verification request emails to Mina Hashimoto (email_marcus_to_mina_receipt_hold, asking her to verify Kevin Tran's $89 with Rachel Whitfield at Vectral) and to Emeka Diallo (email_marcus_to_emeka_receipt_holds, asking him to verify Andre Washington's $89 with Tamara Byrd at Mosaic and Jordan Ekwueme's $45 with Tessa Moreno at BrightLoop). Find AM-forwarded verification emails: Mina to rachel.whitfield@vectralsystems.com (email_mina_to_rachel_receipt_verify), Emeka to tamara.byrd@mosaicrobotics.com (email_emeka_to_tamara_receipt_verify), and Emeka to tessa.moreno@brightloopanalytics.com (email_emeka_to_tessa_receipt_verify). Search for reply emails from Rachel Whitfield, Tamara Byrd, or Tessa Moreno no confirmation replies found, indicating AM verification is still pending on all three.
OE 2: Search emails for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice swap thread using search_emails (query: "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "invoice swap" or "INV-2026-0398" or similar). Discover email threads documenting that on April 10 Hana Kim sent INV-2026-0398 ($4,800) to Raj Greenfield at Sunbelt Venture Partners and INV-2026-0399 ($2,400) to Carmen Delgado-Reyes at Palmetto Community Foundation, but the PDF attachments were swapped Sunbelt's $4,800 invoice PDF went to Palmetto and vice versa. Find complaint emails from Raj (received wrong PDF showing Palmetto header with $2,400) and Carmen (received wrong $4,800 amount). Find Marcus's analysis confirming QuickBooks records are correct and the issue is PDF attachment mismatch creating commercial pricing leakage to a nonprofit client. Find references to the dormant invoice safeguard ticket that was supposed to prevent this. Find corrective emails sent to Raj and Carmen explaining the swap and confirming correct amounts.
OE 3: Search emails for the Heartland overbilling thread using search_emails (query: "Heartland" or "overbilling" or "HM-2026-Q1" or "Jake Loomis" or similar). Discover Chloe Vance's cancellation emails to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com for Lily Marchetti RELOC-2026-009 (email_cancel_marchetti_heartland) and Danielle Osei RELOC-2026-010 (email_cancel_osei_heartland). Find Marcus's formal dispute letter sent to jake.loomis@heartlandmovers.com documenting the $4,800 overbilling on 3 invalid line items with cancellation evidence attached. Search for any reply from Jake Loomis or Heartland to the dispute letter.
OE 4: Search Slack for stipend fraud discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "stipend" or "TechComfort" or "duplicate" or "STIP" or similar). Find in #engineering (C003): Dmitri Volkov's audit messages detailing duplicate SHA-256 hashes for Kevin Tran and Andre Washington ($89 each, TechComfort ErgoKey Pro) and PDF template match for Jordan Ekwueme ($45, TechComfort StandPro Mini), all sharing template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041. Dmitri's announcement of filing ENG-220 (stipend duplicate detection receipt hash fingerprinting). Samira Tariq's technical response about hash dedup before CategoryMatcher and PDF metadata thresholds. Find in #finance (C005): Marcus Thorne's hold notice documenting admin holds on STIP-2026-0094 ($89, Andre Washington) and STIP-2026-0097 ($45, Jordan Ekwueme), noting Kevin Tran's $89 already disbursed 2026-03-08, $134 on hold, $223 potential total loss. Hana Kim's stipend reconciliation tracking entries for the three flagged STIP transactions.
OE 5: Search Slack for Heartland dispute discussions using conversations_search_messages (query: "Heartland" or "HM-2026" or "overbilling" or similar). Find in #operations (C006): Chloe Vance's Heartland Q1 reconciliation messages HM-2026-Q1-0042 for 8 moves at $1,600 each totaling $12,800, due April 25. Only 5 of 8 moves valid (Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves) totaling $8,000 legitimate; $4,800 overbilling on three invalid lines (Marchetti cancelled Feb 10, Osei cancelled Feb 13, Venkatesh handled by Swift not Heartland). Chloe's creation of OPS-342 Linear ticket for reconciliation tracking. Find in #finance (C005): Marcus Thorne's payment hold confirmation and formal dispute summary documenting the $4,800 overcharge with cancellation evidence. Hana Kim's VRECON-2026-Q1-HM tracking entry logging the discrepancy ($12,800 billed, $8,000 verified, $4,800 disputed).
OE 6: Search QuickBooks for stipend-related bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching "STIP" or "stipend" or similar). Find STIP-2026-0089 (bill_stipend_tran_keyboard: Kevin Tran, Vectral Systems, $89, balance $0 paid/disbursed 2026-03-08, account acct_stipend_expense, item "TechComfort ErgoKey Pro", auto-approved by ExpenseBot 2026-03-03). Find STIP-2026-0094 (bill_stipend_washington_keyboard: Andre Washington, Mosaic Robotics, $89, balance $89 pending, due 2026-03-18). Find STIP-2026-0097 (bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor: Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, $45, balance $45 pending, due 2026-03-21). Also find hold entries bill_stipend_washington_keyboard_hold and bill_stipend_ekwueme_monitor_hold confirming administrative holds placed 2026-03-15 by Marcus Thorne, both marked BillableStatus NotBillable. This confirms Kevin Tran's $89 was paid before the fraud investigation started the one payment that already cleared.
OE 7: Search QuickBooks for Heartland bills using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria matching "Heartland" or "HM-2026" or similar). Find bill_heartland_q1_2026 (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042, TotalAmt $12,800, balance $12,800, TxnDate 2026-03-25, DueDate 2026-04-25, vendor: Heartland Movers, 8 line items at $1,600 each covering Abdi, Tran, Kovac, Webb, Reeves, Marchetti, Osei, Venkatesh). Find disputed hold entry bill_heartland_q1_2026_hold (DocNumber HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD, same amounts, SyncToken 1, lines 1-5 marked [VERIFIED], lines 6-8 marked DISPUTED: Marchetti cancelled, Osei cancelled, Venkatesh Swift not Heartland). Valid amount: $8,000 for 5 legitimate moves. Disputed amount: $4,800 for 3 invalid lines.
OE 8: Search QuickBooks for Sunbelt and Palmetto invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria matching "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "INV-2026-039" or similar). Find INV-2026-0398 (Id 398, customer: Sunbelt Venture Partners / cust_sunbelt, TotalAmt $4,800, balance $4,800, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, "April 2026 relocation services Standard package"). Find INV-2026-0399 (Id 399, customer: Palmetto Community Foundation / cust_palmetto, TotalAmt $2,400, balance $2,400, TxnDate 2026-04-10, DueDate 2026-05-10, "April 2026 relocation services Nonprofit package"). QuickBooks amounts are correct the financial exposure is not from incorrect dollar amounts but from the PDF attachment swap that leaked commercial pricing ($4,800) to the nonprofit client and nonprofit pricing ($2,400) to the commercial client.

Find rec_stipend_jordan_monitor_stand (Jordan Ekwueme, BrightLoop Analytics, Monitor Stand, $45, status: Pending Approval, audit flag: PDF Template Match, SHA-256: 7b9e1d3f5a7c9e1b3d5f7a9c1e3b5d7f9a1c3e5b7d9f1a3c5e7b9d1f3a5c7e9b, same template TC-RECEIPT-STD-041, relocation reloc_brightloop_ekwueme). These are the three records the prompt asks to flag for correction.
OE 10: Search Linear for the stipend duplicate detection ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "stipend" or "duplicate" or "ENG-220" or "receipt hash" or similar). Find issue #220 (id: issue_stipend_duplicate_detection), title: "Stipend duplicate detection receipt hash fingerprinting & PDF metadata similarity scoring", assigned to Dmitri Volkov (moveops_dmitri_volkov), priority 1, state: todo (not started), due date 2026-03-25 (overdue by over a month), project: proj_expense_autocategorizer, labels: expense-tool, audit, receipt-fraud, pilot-incident, security. Description covers all three flagged transactions, proposed DuplicateDetector pipeline (Layer 1 hash comparison, Layer 2 PDF metadata, Layer 3 cross-client dedup), $223 total exposure, CEO $100 manual verification directive. This is "Dmitri's duplicate detection work" referenced in the prompt still in todo, never started.
OE 11: Search Linear for the invoice safeguard ticket using linear_list_issues (query: "invoice" or "safeguard" or "template" or "attachment mismatch" or similar). This is "the safeguard ticket engineering was supposed to build" it was never deployed, and its absence directly enabled the Sunbelt/Palmetto PDF swap incident on April 10.
